title: Cosmos Stack — drop sliding blocks to build a glowing tower
summary: A real, self-contained Stack/tower arcade game in a single HTML file with zero dependencies. A block slides back and forth above your tower; you drop it onto the stack and any overhang is sliced off, so the block shrinks each time you misalign. Perfect drops keep full width and flash a highlight, the tower scrolls down as it rises, and missing the stack entirely topples the tower and ends the run. Playable with keyboard and touch. It also ships a deterministic self-playing attract demo for capture: a seeded AI drops blocks with near-perfect timing onto an ever-rising tower, rendered as a pure function of time via window.__seek(t).
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html. CONTROLS: SPACE or tap/click anywhere on the board to drop the sliding block; R key or the Restart button to start over. GOAL: stack as high as possible — score = tower height (one point per block placed); overhang is sliced off so the block shrinks, and dropping with zero overlap topples the tower (game over). KEY TECHNIQUES: the game is a fixed-timestep (16ms) deterministic simulation; the moving block position is a pure sine function of time with a mulberry32-seeded per-level phase and direction, so AUTO replays identically; the seeded auto-player drops each block aligned to the top block's center with a tiny deterministic jitter so blocks visibly shrink over time; AUTO re-simulates from a fixed initial state to t each seek (N small) so the frame is a pure function of time, with a START offset so frame 0 already shows a tall mid-build tower with score>0. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: BH, BASEW, SPEED, AMP, DROP_INTERVAL, the auto jitter range, and LOOP.
